summarization.a: The line chart shows the atomic percentage of hydrogen (H Content) and carbon (C Content) as a function of processing temperature. The atomic percentage of H decreases as the processing temperature increases, while the atomic percentage of C remains almost constant.
summarization.b: The line chart illustrates the change in conductivity (Cond) with respect to processing temperature. The Li-ion conductivity decreases as the processing temperature increases.

a.question: What type of samples are being analyzed?
a.answer: Li3PO4 films with a thickness of 40 nm.

b.question: How does conductivity change as processing temperature increases?
b.answer: The Li-ion conductivity decreases when the processing temperature increases.

b.question: At what processing temperature is the conductivity highest?
b.answer: The li-ion conductivity is highest at 200 °C.

b.question: Why does the conductivity of Li-ion decrease when the processing temperature is higher?
b.answer: The Li-ion conductivity decreases at higher temperatures due to increased crystallinity of the layers.
